description: Comprehensive API workflows for testing all HTTP methods and object hierarchies in the TMI OpenAPI specification

oauth_init.description: Initiate OAuth authorization code flow with PKCE

parameters.login_hint: optional - login hint for test provider (3-20 chars, alphanumeric + hyphens)

oauth_callback.description: Handle OAuth callback with authorization code

oauth_token_exchange.description: Exchange authorization code for access and refresh tokens using PKCE
oauth_token_exchange.returns: JWT access_token and refresh_token for Bearer authentication

exchangeOAuthCode.description: Exchange OAuth authorization code for JWT tokens

deleteUserAccount.description: Delete user account and all data

updateWebhookDeliveryStatus.description: Update webhook delivery status (for webhook listeners)

migrateSystemSettings.description: Migrate settings from config files to database

listAdminUserClientCredentials.description: List client credentials for a user

createAdminUserClientCredential.description: Create client credential for a user

deleteAdminUserClientCredential.description: Delete client credential for a user

getIntakeSurveyResponseMetadataByKey.description: Get survey response metadata by key

updateIntakeSurveyResponseMetadataByKey.description: Update survey response metadata by key

deleteIntakeSurveyResponseMetadataByKey.description: Delete survey response metadata by key

listIntakeSurveyResponseTriageNotes.description: List triage notes for survey response

getTriageSurveyResponseMetadataByKey.description: Get triage response metadata by key

createThreatModelFromSurveyResponse.description: Promote survey response to threat model

listTimmyChatSessions.description: List chat sessions for a threat model

listTimmyChatMessages.description: List chat messages in a session

listSAMLUsers.description: List SAML users for a provider

list.description: List threats in a threat model
List documents in a threat model
List sources in a threat model

get.description: Get threat model metadata by key

set_key.description: Update threat model metadata by key

delete_key.description: Delete threat model metadata by key

list.description: List notes in a threat model
List assets in a threat model
Get all metadata for an asset

get.description: Get specific metadata for an asset

threat_model_full_crud.description: Start OAuth authorization flow with PKCE
Exchange code for access token using code_verifier

notes.oauth_flow: The API uses OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE (RFC 7636). Requires: (1) /oauth2/authorize to get code, (2) /oauth2/token to exchange code+verifier for tokens.
notes.authentication: All endpoints under /threat_models, /admin, /intake, /triage, /teams, /projects, /me, /addons, and /config require Bearer token authentication using JWT tokens obtained from the OAuth flow.
notes.hierarchies: The API follows hierarchical structure: threat_model -> {threats, diagrams, documents, assets, notes, repositories} -> metadata. Child objects cannot exist without parent objects. Audit trails and restore are available for all hierarchical resources.
notes.websockets: Real-time collaboration available for diagrams via WebSocket at /ws/diagrams/{id} endpoint (not included in REST API workflow).
notes.bulk_operations: Many endpoints support bulk operations (POST/PUT/PATCH /bulk) for efficiency with multiple objects.
notes.metadata_pattern: All primary objects support metadata operations: list, create, get_key, set_key, delete_key, bulk_create/bulk_replace/bulk_upsert.
notes.pkce_security: PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) is mandatory for OAuth flows. Generate code_verifier (43-128 chars), compute code_challenge = base64url(sha256(code_verifier)).
notes.survey_workflow: Surveys follow a lifecycle: admin creates survey -> intake submits responses -> triage reviews and promotes to threat model.
notes.admin_operations: Admin endpoints under /admin/ cover users, groups, quotas, settings, webhooks, surveys, and Timmy AI management.
notes.saml_authentication: SAML 2.0 is supported as an alternative to OAuth, with endpoints for SSO login, ACS, SLO, and SP metadata.
notes.audit_and_restore: All threat model resources support soft-delete with restore capability, plus audit trails with rollback.
notes.client_credentials: Machine-to-machine authentication via client credentials grant (RFC 6749 Section 4.4), manageable via /me/ and /admin/ endpoints.
